title: Replace Deprecated FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING in EntityRelationshipController
description:
The deprecated PHP constant `FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING` was generating warning logs every second in the `EntityRelationshipController` (line 19). While not user-impacting, the warning was clogging New Relic logs and masking other errors.

## Affected File

`app/Http/Controllers/EntityRelationshipController.php` (line 19)

## Observed Behavior

New Relic captured constant WARNING-level log entries: "Constant FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING is deprecated" on every request to the entity relationships endpoint.

## Fix

Replaced `FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING` with `htmlentities()` for input sanitization.
acceptance_criteria: No deprecation warnings for FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING appear in New Relic logs
Input sanitization continues to function correctly using htmlentities()
Entity relationship endpoint behavior is unchanged
Log noise from this deprecation is eliminated
middleware_changes: Replaced deprecated `FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING` constant with `htmlentities()` in EntityRelationshipController for PHP 8.x compatibility.
implementation_notes: PHP 8.1 deprecated FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING. The replacement with htmlentities() provides equivalent sanitization while eliminating the deprecation warning.
comment_summary: Developer replaced FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING with htmlentities() to resolve the deprecation warning.
